DDR5-6400.reason: NEMIX $1,488 Day 32 longest anchor in tracking history (63 consecutive verifications). DDR5 spot May +5.2% strongest monthly performance. DRAM Q2 +58-63% on top of Q1 +90-95% – massive cumulative. SK Hynix chairman: shortage until 2030. 7-source band for 64GB validated. No waiting rationale.

DDR5-5600.reason: DDR5 spot 4-consecutive ATH $41.900 (May +5.2%). 96GB NEMIX $3,200 Day 32 second month anchor with 6-source cross-validation. DDR4 concurrent rally (3 weeks+) validates full DRAM bull market – no substitution path. Server DRAM Q2 +58-63% > PC DDR5 +43-48% – AI datacenter demand dominant.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: URGENCY UPGRADED: Samsung PM1733 officially discontinued – 7.68TB low-end anchor supply contracting irreversibly. NAND Q2 +70-75% FIRST TIME overtaking DRAM Q2 +58-63% – storage bull center shifting to NAND. Phison CEO warns enterprise shutdowns. NAND MLC 17 consecutive monthly records. DiscTech offline 8 weeks – tracking lag compiling. All chains sold out Day 35. Goldman Sachs 4.2% NAND deficit Day 10. Micron 6600 ION 245TB shipping confirms QLC demand trajectory. No waiting justification exists.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: 30.72TB 5-week+ sustained price increase (SS $17,700 Day 24 +6.6% from $16,600). SHI $21,529 (+10.0%) new upper band confirms premium-tier pricing accelerating. CDW-G In Stock 'estimated delivery today' Day 5. PM9D3a bottleneck Day 13 reduces competitive pressure. Brand 3-tier structure Day 20. Provantage 7.68TB $5,242 dual-confirmed with CDW – high-end band solid.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Mouser 61.44TB Day 12 only 1 unit – structural crisis deepening. But Connection/GovConnection alternative channels Day 5 sustained – procurement viable at 34-37% premium. Newegg $29,950 high-end pricing expanding. 321-layer QLC H2 2026 + Micron 6600 ION 245TB = QLC demand trajectory confirmed but no 2026 supply relief. 6500 ION EOL complete. Procure now at premium channels.

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Q2 +70-75% OVERTAKING DRAM Q2 +58-63% – storage bull center shifting
escalatedRisks.status: NEW SIGNAL – Day 1. NAND price growth rate now exceeds DRAM for first time in current cycle.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND Q2 contract +70-75% QoQ first time exceeding DRAM Q2 +58-63%. NAND MLC 128Gb $26.51 (+9.73% MoM) 17th consecutive monthly record. Phison CEO warns shortages could shut down entire consumer electronics companies. Storage bull market center of gravity shifting from DRAM to NAND.

escalatedRisks.status: NEW – May 2026. Samsung Semiconductor official. PM1733 PCIe4 TLC 7.68TB low-end anchor ($1,390-$3,500) supply contracting permanently.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung PM1733 (PCIe Gen4 enterprise TLC SSD series) officially discontinued. Future supply will increasingly depend on refurb/secondary market. PM1733a (upgraded Gen4) and PM1743 (Gen5) are replacement paths but at higher price points.

escalatedRisks.risk: SHI Micron 9550 Pro 30.72TB upper band breakthrough $21,529
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – +$1,956 (+10.0%) from previous SHI reference $19,573. Premium-tier pricing accelerating.
escalatedRisks.detail: SHI Micron 9550 Pro 30.72TB now listed at $21,529 (was $19,573). Combined with SS $17,700 (Day 24 +6.6%) and CDW-G $17,274.70 In Stock, the 6-reference band is widening upward.

escalatedRisks.risk: Phison CEO: NAND shortages could shut down consumer electronics companies
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – Extreme signal. Phison is world largest independent NAND controller supplier.
escalatedRisks.detail: Phison CEO K.S. Pua warned NAND shortages severe enough to shut down entire consumer electronics companies in 2026. At least one foundry demanding three-year cash payments upfront.

escalatedRisks.risk: 321-layer QLC NAND H2 2026 shipment – distant supply dawn
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – Positive signal but NO 2026 impact. 67% density increase vs current 192-layer.
escalatedRisks.detail: Multiple NAND suppliers plan 321-layer QLC NAND shipments starting H2 2026. 2026 procurement decisions should NOT be delayed based on this. Buy now at current premium, 321-layer QLC benefits materialize 2027-2028.

escalatedRisks.risk: DRAM Q2 contract slowing to +58-63% – high base effect, NOT price peak
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – Clarification signal. Deceleration from Q1 +90-95% is normal bull market behavior.
escalatedRisks.detail: DRAM Q2 contract projected +58-63% QoQ vs Q1 +90-95%. Server DRAM (+58-63%) still outpacing PC DDR5 (+43-48%). ABSOLUTE price increase in Q2 may exceed Q1 due to higher base. Typical Phase 2 bull market.

escalatedRisks.risk: Micron 6600 ION 245TB shipping – QLC nearline era begins 🆕🆕 (SECOND PASS)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – World's highest-capacity commercially available SSD now shipping. Micron G9 QLC NAND, PCIe5, U.2+E3.L. AI data lakes, cloud-scale object storage.
escalatedRisks.source: Micron official / GlobeNewswire / TechPowerUp / ServeTheHome May 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Micron 6600 ION 245TB officially shipping as of May 2026. 4x capacity jump from D5-P5336 61.44TB. PCIe5 QLC form factor. Each unit consumes massive NAND (equivalent to 4x D5-P5336 61.44TB or 2x 122.88TB). Targeting hyperscaler AI data lakes and cloud-scale file/object storage. This confirms QLC demand trajectory is structural, not temporary. If hyperscaler adoption accelerates, QLC NAND consumption could surpass TLC NAND by 2028-2029.
categoriesAffected: PCIe5 QLC (new category, not currently tracked)
escalatedRisks.confidence: high (official Micron press release + multiple media confirmations)

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung 250TB-1PB nearline SSD roadmap with Scality 🆕 (SECOND PASS)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – 2027 target. Co-development with Scality. New flash type with lower endurance than QLC for read-intensive workloads. EDSFF E3.L/E2 form factors.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung developing 250TB to 1PB nearline SSDs with Scality for dense storage racks. New flash type optimized for cost/density rather than endurance → read-intensive workloads (AI datasets, media archives, RAG). 2027 target release. Combined with Micron 6600 ION 245TB (2026) and 321-layer QLC (2028+), the nearline all-flash roadmap is clearly defined. Implication: HDD replacement by QLC/ultra-dense flash SSDs is accelerating, which will drive sustained NAND demand growth through at least 2030.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium (Samsung/Scality co-development confirmed, timeline subject to fab readiness)

escalatedRisks.risk: Provantage Micron 9550 Pro 7.68TB $5,242 – dual-source confirmation 🆕 (SECOND PASS)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW – Cross-validates CDW $5,241.99. Micron 9550 Pro 7.68TB high-band now dual-sourced, strengthening PCIe5 TLC 7.68TB upper-end credibility.
escalatedRisks.detail: Provantage and CDW independently show Micron 9550 Pro 7.68TB at ~$5,242. Dual-source confirmation eliminates single-source noise risk. Micron 9550 Pro now has firm dual-sourced pricing at 7.68TB (brand premium tier within the Samsung $1,800-$4,400 / Kioxia $1,402-$4,348 / Micron $2,250-$5,242 three-tier structure).
